title: Leo's Wild Survival
subtitle:
A Story About Staying Calm
and Finding Your Way Home
author: Written & Illustrated for Andrew

pages.title: The Rabbit
pages.text:
Leo's family was camping at Pine Lake for the first time. While Dad set up the tent and Mom unpacked the cooler, Leo spotted a rabbit — small, brown, with a twitchy nose.

"Come here, little guy!" he whispered, and crawled after it into the trees. The rabbit hopped. Leo followed. Deeper and deeper into the green shadows.

pages.title: Lost
pages.text:
The rabbit disappeared. Leo looked around — the trees all looked the same. "Hello? Hello!" No answer. His heart started beating fast. The sun was going down, and the forest was getting darker.

He sat down under a big oak tree and hugged his knees. He was lost.

pages.title: STOP
pages.text:
Then he remembered what Dad always said: "If you're lost, STOP. Sit down, Think, Observe, and Plan." Leo took a deep breath. He had a whistle on his backpack strap. He blew three short blasts.

No answer. "Okay," he said to himself. "Plan B."

pages.title: The Stream
pages.text:
He found a small stream. Dad had told him that water flows downhill, and downhill leads to people. He followed the stream, stepping carefully over wet rocks.

A deer watched him from the other side. "Hi, deer," Leo said. The deer dipped its head and walked away.

pages.title: Shelter
pages.text:
He came to a clearing — flat ground, soft moss, a fallen tree. He decided to make a shelter.

He leaned branches against the fallen tree to make a roof, then covered them with ferns and leaves. Inside, he spread dry moss to sleep on. It wasn't a hotel, but it was safe.

pages.title: Wild Food
pages.text:
Leo was hungry. He remembered that dandelions are edible — the whole plant. He found some near the stream, washed them, and ate the leaves.

They were bitter, but his stomach stopped growling. "Wild salad," he said, and almost laughed.

pages.title: Fire
pages.text:
The night was cold. Leo found clear ground, circled it with stones, and gathered dry twigs and bark. He took the lens from his sunglasses and focused sunlight onto dry bark.

Nothing happened. Then — a tiny curl of smoke. He blew gently. A flame.

pages.title: Night
pages.text:
The fire crackled and popped, sending orange sparks into the dark sky. Leo added bigger sticks. The warmth felt like a hug.

He sat by the fire, watching the stars between the treetops, and sang his favorite campfire song quietly. He wasn't scared anymore — he was a survivor.

pages.title: HELP
pages.text:
Morning. Leo woke to birds singing. He put more wood on the fire to keep smoke rising. Then he used his shoes to make big letters in the clearing:

H E L P — visible from the sky. He sat down next to the letters and waited.

pages.title: Rescue
pages.text:
A low hum grew louder. A helicopter appeared over the treetops! Leo waved his red jacket and blew his whistle as loud as he could.

The helicopter hovered, then slowly came down. A rescue worker jumped out, smiling. "Good job, kid. You did everything right."

pages.title: Reunion
pages.text:
Back at camp, Mom burst into tears and hugged him so tight he couldn't breathe. Dad held him for a long time. "I'm proud of you," Dad said. "You remembered everything we practiced."

Leo felt warm inside. He was a boy who survived.

pages.title: Home
pages.text:
At home, Leo pinned a paper on his bedroom wall:

LEO'S WILDERNESS RULES
1. STOP — sit and think
2. Find water and follow it
3. Make shelter before dark
4. Fire = signal
5. Be brave, you're stronger than you know

He smiled. He couldn't wait for the next camping trip.

vocabulary.en: A place that protects you from weather
To stay in one place in the air

questions.q: Why did Leo get lost in the forest?
opts: A. He wanted to explore the forest alone
B. He followed a rabbit into the woods
C. He ran away from his parents
D. He got on the wrong trail
questions.text: He followed a rabbit into the woods
questions.exp: Leo saw a rabbit and crawled after it into the trees (page 1). He was just curious, not trying to run away.

questions.q: What did Leo do FIRST when he realized he was lost?
opts: A. He built a shelter
B. He made a fire
C. He blew his whistle and called for help
D. He followed a stream
questions.text: He blew his whistle and called for help
questions.exp: Leo first blew three short blasts on his whistle and called out (page 3). Only when nobody answered did he move to Plan B.

questions.q: Why did Leo follow the stream?
opts: A. He was thirsty
B. He wanted to catch fish for dinner
C. His dad told him water flows downhill to people
D. The deer was drinking from it
questions.text: His dad told him water flows downhill to people
questions.exp: Leo remembered his dad's advice: water flows downhill, and downhill leads to people (page 4).

questions.q: What does "shelter" mean in the story?
opts: A. A warm blanket
B. A place that protects you from weather
C. A type of food
D. A rescue helicopter
questions.text: A place that protects you from weather
questions.exp: Leo built a shelter using branches, ferns, and leaves to protect himself from the cold night air (page 5).

questions.q: The story says the dandelions were "edible." What does edible mean?
opts: A. Poisonous and dangerous
B. Safe to eat
C. Bitter and bad-tasting
D. Very expensive
questions.exp: Edible means safe to eat. Leo knew dandelions are edible - the whole plant can be eaten (page 6).

questions.q: How did Leo start the fire?
opts: A. He used matches from his pocket
B. He rubbed two sticks together
C. He used the lens from his sunglasses to focus sunlight
D. He found a lighter in the woods
questions.text: He used the lens from his sunglasses to focus sunlight
questions.exp: Leo took the lens out of his sunglasses and focused sunlight onto dry bark until it started smoking (page 7).

questions.q: The story says Leo used "ferns" for his shelter. What are ferns?
opts: A. Small rocks
B. A type of green plant with feathery leaves
C. Pieces of clothing
D. Tree branches
questions.text: A type of green plant with feathery leaves
questions.exp: Ferns are green plants with feathery, divided leaves that grow in forests (page 5).

questions.q: What did Leo do to help rescuers find him in the morning?
opts: A. He climbed a tall tree
B. He made a HELP sign with his shoes and kept the fire smoking
C. He walked back the way he came
D. He called 911 on a phone
questions.text: He made a HELP sign with his shoes and kept the fire smoking
questions.exp: Leo kept the fire smoking and used his shoes to make the word HELP in the clearing, visible from the sky (page 9).

questions.q: The helicopter "hovered" before landing. What does hovered mean?
opts: A. It landed very fast
B. It stayed in one place in the air
C. It made a loud noise
D. It flew away
questions.text: It stayed in one place in the air
questions.exp: To hover means to stay suspended in one place in the air. The helicopter hovered before coming down (page 10).

questions.q: What is the MAIN lesson Leo learned from his experience?
opts: A. Never go camping again
B. Always stay with your family, and if you get lost, stay calm and use survival skills
C. Rabbits are dangerous animals
D. The forest is a scary place
questions.text: Stay with family, stay calm, use survival skills
questions.exp: Leo's rules at the end show the main lesson: stay calm, use survival skills, and be brave (page 12). He was excited for the next trip, not scared.
